text: Rama, hearing the words of his father King Dasharatha, accepted the exile to the forest for fourteen years without any resentment. He considered it his duty to uphold his father's honor.
translation: Rama heard his father's command and bowed his head in acceptance. He said, 'I shall go to the forest willingly. I seek only your blessings.'

text: During their exile, Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana encountered many sages in the forest who shared wisdom about dharma, patience, and the purpose of life.
translation: The sages taught that adversity is a teacher, and that forest life with its challenges builds character and spiritual strength.

text: Jambavan reminded Hanuman of his divine powers, saying, 'You are the son of Vayu, the wind god. You possess strength beyond imagination. Why do you doubt yourself?'
translation: Hanuman, upon hearing this, remembered his true nature. His body swelled with energy and he grew to a tremendous size, ready to leap across the ocean.

text: Hanuman leaped across the vast ocean, demonstrating that faith and self-belief can overcome any obstacle. During his flight, mountains rose to offer him rest, and the ocean creatures respected his mission.
translation: Hanuman flew across the ocean with unwavering focus, his mind fixed only on finding Sita and delivering Rama's message of hope.

text: Vibhishana, though born a rakshasa, chose righteousness over family loyalty. He advised Ravana to return Sita, and when his advice was rejected, he joined Rama's side.
translation: Vibhishana said, 'I choose dharma over adharma, truth over falsehood, and righteousness over family ties. This is the path of wisdom.'
